news.summary: Kazakhstan aims to become a prominent crypto hub in Central Asia by relaxing regulatory restrictions, as suggested by Kanysh Tuleushin, the country's first vice minister of digital development. Tuleushin believes that digital mining and policy shifts could establish Kazakhstan as a leader in blockchain technology. He emphasizes the potential impact of lifting restrictions, citing the potential addition of hundreds of billions of tenge to the national budget. Tuleushin advocates for broader legalization, taxation, and implementation of clear crypto regulations, transparent exchanges, and legal crypto ATMs. Binance's CZ signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Kazakhstan in 2023, showing external interest in the nation's crypto potential. Furthermore, crypto mining companies are seen as potential drivers of infrastructure modernization in Kazakhstan, similar to their role in the US in balancing power grids by consuming surplus energy. Tuleushin's 70/30 energy initiative suggests involving foreign investors in upgrading thermal power plants, with the generated capacity being split 70%-30% between the national grid and miners, respectively. He also proposes utilizing associated petroleum gas to power data centers, reducing emissions and creating revenue for oil producers. The country's crypto mining sector has already contributed $34.6 million in taxes over three years, with 415,000 mining machines registered and licenses issued for mining pools. The AIFC exchange saw a surge in crypto trading from $324 million in 2023 to $1.4 billion in 2024, with plans for miners to sell 75% of their assets through AIFC platforms beginning in 2025.Despite these advancements, unregulated crypto transactions persist outside the AIFC, with an estimated $4.1 billion turnover in 2023, mostly beyond government oversight. In 2024, 36 illegal exchanges were shut down, freezing $4.8 million in assets and disrupting two Ponzi schemes. Additionally, Kazakhstan is developing a central bank digital currency, the digital tenge, with plans initially set for launch in 2025. Fellow Central Asian countries Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan are also adopting friendly approaches toward the digital asset industry. Binance signed an MOU with Kyrgyzstan's National Agency for Investments in May 2023 to introduce crypto payment infrastructure and blockchain education in the country.
news.headline: Kazakhstan aims to be Central Asia's top crypto hub with eased regulations, as mining tax revenue hits $34.6M in 3 years. Source: CZ #crypto

news.summary: Major financial firms have revised their U.S. recession predictions following a 90-day tariff pause between the U.S. and China. According to recent reports, Goldman Sachs has decreased the likelihood of a recession from 45% to 35%, while Barclays has completely ruled out the possibility. J.P. Morgan now estimates the chances to be less than 50%. This shift in forecasts occurred after both countries agreed to lower tariffs - the U.S. reduced duties on Chinese imports from 145% to 30%, and China lowered rates on U.S. goods from 125% to 10%. In addition, Goldman has raised its GDP forecast for 2025 by 0.5 points to 1%. For more details, refer to the full story on the Benzinga website.
news.headline: Major brokerages like Goldman Sachs, Barclays, and J.P. Morgan retract US recession forecasts post 90-day US-China tariff truce. Odds of U.S. recession reduced: Goldman Sachs down to 35%, Barclays rules out a recession, J.P. Morgan predicts less than 50%. Revised forecasts due to tariff reductions: US duties on Chinese imports down from 145% to 30%, China's rates on US goods lowered from 125% to 10%. Goldman raises 2025 GDP forecast by 0.5 points to 1%. Less Fed cuts expected.

news.summary: AngloGold Ashanti plc reports that Executive Director Alberto Calderon and Executive Officer Richard Jordinson have conducted transactions involving the Company's securities. Calderon sold a portion of the shares received to cover taxes. Jordinson, set to retire as Chief Operating Officer, also received shares. AngloGold Ashanti plc is listed on NYSE (AU) and JSE (ANG). news.summary: The SEC has postponed its decision on the proposed spot Solana ETF to October 2025, along with similar decisions on other cryptocurrencies like Litecoin, Polkadot, XRP, and Dogecoin. The introduction of spot ETFs like the US spot Bitcoin ETFs, which captured 75% of new investments after launching, plays a significant role in enhancing liquidity and institutional adoption of digital assets. Despite delays, investors remain positive about the approval of a SOL ETF before the end of 2025, with an 82% approval chance predicted. Other crypto ETF applications for Polkadot, XRP, and Dogecoin are approaching SEC deadlines in June, with decisions expected around the 11th, 17th, and 24th. The SEC has a history of utilizing its full 240-day review period for evaluating crypto-related financial products.
news.headline: SEC postpones Solana ETF decision to Oct 2025. Polkadot, XRP ETFs await SEC ruling in June. Investors optimistic about ETF approval.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures surged over 2% in premarket trading following the announcement of a 90-day truce between the U.S. and China, reducing reciprocal tariffs from 125% to 10%. Trade negotiations over the weekend in Geneva showed progress, with the U.S. maintaining 20% tariffs on Chinese imports related to fentanyl, keeping the overall tariff level at 30%. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.44%, while the two-year bond was at 3.98%. CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 92.1% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in June. Key futures changes include Dow Jones (+2.12%), S&P 500 (+2.76%), Nasdaq 100 (+3.68%), and Russell 2000 (+3.16%). ETFs tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 also showed increase in premarket trading. Notable sector movements and specific company performances were seen on Friday. EchoStar Corp. saw a rise in sales while Plains All American Pipeline LP reported a decline in revenue. The Dow Jones index closed at 41,249.38, down 119 points or 0.29%.
news.headline: US stock futures rise over 2% after US and China agree to 90-day tariff pause, reducing tariffs from 125% to 10%. Treasury bonds yields at 4.44% and 3.98%. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged in June. Dow Jones up 2.12%, S&P 500 up 2.76%, Nasdaq 100 up 3.68%, Russell 2000 up 3.16%. SPY up 2.74% to $579.80, QQQ up 3.81% to $506.54. Energy, real estate, consumer discretionary, and materials sectors advanced, while health care, communication services, consumer staples, and information technology sectors declined. EchoStar Corp. rises 1.43%, Plains All American Pipeline LP falls nearly 3%.

news.summary: A recent economic analysis by Cebr indicates that the Government's implementation of price caps could lead to negative repercussions, such as an increase in empty seats, a rise in fraudulent activities, and a reduction in consumer spending. These consequences pose significant risks for the economy and consumer market.
news.headline: Introduction of ticket price caps in UK could cost economy £180 million, leading to empty seats and rising fraud.

According to Mani Vembu, CEO of Zoho, traditional CRMs have primarily been limited to sales departments, despite multiple organizational units requiring access to customer data. This move addresses this issue with innovative solutions.

This integrated solution allows businesses to handle transactions seamlessly within the Zoho ecosystem. news.summary: In Sunnyvale, California, Avicena, a pioneer in ultra-low power high-density microLED-based interconnects, revealed that they have secured $65 million in Series B Funding. This funding round, spearheaded by Tiger Global and joined by Maverick Silicon, Prosperity7 Ventures, Venture Tech Alliance, SK hynix, Cerberus Capital Management, Hitachi Ventures, and Lam Research, has propelled their total capital raised to $120 million. The funding round was highly oversubscribed, indicating strong investor interest in the company's innovative technology.
news.headline: Avicena secures $65M in Series B funding led by Tiger Global, bringing total raised to $120M with SK hynix participation.

news.summary: Solana (SOL) experienced a slight decline in the early hours of Wednesday after reaching a nearly three-month high. The cryptocurrency surged to $184.72, its highest point since Feb. 18, before settling around $180. Over the past 24 hours, SOL saw a growth of over 5%, although its trading volume decreased by 4.97%. Investments in SOL derivatives also increased significantly, with Open Interest in futures and options climbing by 9% to $7.48 billion, the highest level since Jan. 23, as reported by Coinglass. In addition, data from Binance revealed that more than 63% of derivatives traders with open SOL positions were positioned long.
news.headline: Solana (SOL) hits 3-month high of $184.72, up over 5% in last 24 hours. Derivatives market sees $7.48 billion open interest.

news.summary: European Wax Center, Inc. reported financial results for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 compared to fiscal 2024. Net new centers increased by 1.0% to 1,062 centers in 45 states, with system-wide sales reaching $225.9 million, a 2.1% increase. Total revenue was $51.4 million, a 0.9% decrease, while same-store sales saw a 0.7% increase. GAAP net income decreased by 29.7% to $2.6 million, and adjusted net income increased by 10.3% to $9.5 million. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 7.2% to $18.8 million. The company aims for net unit growth by the end of 2026. The balance sheet showed $58.3 million in cash and cash equivalents, $6.5 million in restricted cash, $389.0 million in borrowings outstanding, and no outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility. The fiscal 2025 outlook includes system-wide sales of $940 million to $960 million, total revenue of $210 million to $214 million, same-store sales growth of 0.0% to 2.0%, adjusted net income of $31 million to $33 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $69 million to $71 million. In the second quarter, 1 center opened and 2 closed. Euro Wax Center operates over 1,000 centers and generated $951 million in sales in fiscal 2024. A forward-looking statement regarding expected financial performance, strategy, and growth prospects for the company was presented. Non-GAAP financial measures are included to evaluate operating performance. The company's balance sheet, liabilities, and stockholders' equity were also detailed.
news.headline: European Wax Center, Inc. reports Q1 fiscal 2025 results: 1,062 total centers, $225.9M system-wide sales, $51.4M total revenue. Adjusted net income $9.5M, up 10.3%. Adjusted EBITDA $18.8M, up 7.2%. Plano, TX. CEO Morris reiterates full-year outlook and plans for growth. New centers opened-5, closed-10. Repurchased approx 0.2M shares for $1.1M. Ending cash-$58.3M. Fiscal 2025 outlook: System-wide sales $940M-$960M, total revenue $210M-$214M. Net new center openings-10 to 12, closures-40 to 60 planned. 1 center opened/2 closed in Q2. Webcast conference call held, more info at https://investors.waxcenter.com. Non-GAAP financial measures included. Outlook remains positive amidst challenges. Source: Benzinga.

news.summary: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reported their financial results for the three months ended March 31, 2025. Compared to the same period in 2024, the Corporation saw a 51.2% increase in total sales volumes to 68,344 hectoliters, up from 45,204 hectoliters, driven by a significant increase in contract sales volumes. Net revenue increased by 30.5% to $11.2 million from $8.6 million, with gross margin up to 33.1% from 20.2%. Operating income improved to $0.1 million from a loss of $(2.3) million, and the net loss decreased by $3.1 million to $nil. Adjusted EBITDA rose by $2.2 million to $0.7 million. Sales volumes breakdown shows wholesale sales at 31,892 hectoliters and contract sales at 36,452 hectoliters. Gross product revenue was $14,203, and cost of sales was $7,491. Net loss per share for the quarter was $0.00 compared to $0.44 in the same quarter of the previous year. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reports a 51.2% increase in sales volumes, with gross margin up to 33.1% and net revenue at $11.2 million.

news.summary: Boyd Group Services Inc. announced their first quarter results for 2025, with a 1.0% decrease in sales to $778.3 million and a 2.8% drop in same-store sales. Gross profit increased by 1.9% to $359.3 million, with Adjusted EBITDA decreasing by 1.4% to $80.5 million. Adjusted net earnings were $2.2 million, down from $9.4 million in 2024. Debt increased to $510.4 million, and they declared a C$0.153 dividend per share. Boyd added nine collision repair locations and two glass business acquisitions. They implemented cost-saving measures and added three more collision repair locations post-quarter end. The CEO highlighted market share gains and a gross margin increase to 46.2%. Market headwinds were acknowledged, but the company is optimistic about their performance and progress on Project 360.
news.headline: Boyd Group Services Inc. reports Q1 2025 results: Sales down 1% to $778.3M, gross profit up 1.9% to $359.3M. Adjusted EBITDA down 1.4% to $80.5M, net loss of $2.6M. Debt increased to $510.4M. Added 9 locations. CEO notes market share gains and improved margins. Staffing model change to save $30M annually. Subsequent to quarter end, added 3 locations.

news.summary: TerraVest Industries Inc. reported its financial results for the second quarter ending March 31, 2025, with sales at $311,450 and $546,035 for the quarter and six months, respectively. Net income for the same periods was $33,349 and $63,780. Adjusted EBITDA saw a significant increase at $65,687 for the quarter and $114,587 for the six months. The company made various acquisitions in recent years, impacting its sales performance. Excluding these acquisitions, sales increased by 14% for the quarter but decreased by 1% for the six months for the base portfolio. The increase in sales was due to growth in HVAC and Containment Equipment segments, domestic gas tanks, and certain gas transportation products. The company attributed its boost in net income to acquisitions, increased base portfolio sales, favorable product mix, and a gain on bargain purchase. Foreign exchange gains also contributed to the rise in net income.
news.headline: TerraVest Industries Inc. announced Q2 results with sales at $311,450 (a 45% increase) and net income at $33,349 (a 30% increase). Acquisitions contributed to sales growth. Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 was $65,687.

news.summary: Renting in major cities like Miami, New York, Los Angeles, Boston, and San Diego proves to be challenging due to high costs, making them the least affordable markets for renters. In contrast, Oklahoma City in Oklahoma, Austin in Texas, Columbus in Ohio, Raleigh in North Carolina, and Minneapolis in Minnesota offer more affordable options for renters. There has been a surge in new construction for multi-family properties, helping to alleviate rental prices in some areas.
news.headline: Renters spent 23.4% on rent in April; Miami, NY, LA, Boston, and San Diego least affordable, while Oklahoma City, Austin, Columbus, Raleigh, and Minneapolis most affordable; surge in multi-family construction easing rent.

This program is in accordance with the recent executive order by President Trump, announced on May 5, 2025. The decision was unveiled at the Saudi-U.S. Investment Forum.
news.headline: Shamekh SIV Solutions to invest $5.8B for new pharmaceutical manufacturing plants globally post-Trump's executive order.

news.summary: The establishment of an office in the Middle East demonstrates Kimmeridge's dedication to the region, solidifying its position as a pivotal hub for international energy investments focused on cost efficiency. This move comes after an agreement was reached with Mubadala Energy for the acquisition of a minority interest in Kimmeridge's SoTex assets.

news.summary: Plantae Technologies Inc., a Canadian company specializing in sustainable fiber products, has secured a deal to become the sole provider of geometrically refined hemp bast fibers for Zylotex Inc., a global leader in cellulosic fibers. This partnership was announced on May 14, 2025, in Edmonton, AB. Plantae Technologies will exclusively supply these specialized fibers to Zylotex, further solidifying their position in the market. This collaboration is set to enhance the production capabilities of Zylotex with high-quality hemp bast fibers, paving the way for continued growth and success.
news.headline: Plantae Technologies and Zylotex sign exclusive agreement for hemp fibre-based Lyocell production, making Plantae the sole supplier of refined hemp bast fibers for Zylotex. (Characters: 144)

news.summary: Wishpond Technologies Ltd., a company specializing in marketing-focused online business solutions, based in Vancouver, BC, has announced that it anticipates releasing its First Quarter Financial Statements. Trading on TSXV under the symbol WISH and on OTCQX under the symbol WPNDF, the company remains optimistic about its performance in the coming months.
news.headline: Wishpond to announce first quarter 2025 financial results via video call on May 14, 2025. TSXV: WISH, OTCQX: WPNDF.
